content.text: These sculpted flower brooches are built from individual petals laid around a central bead, a technique called
petal layering

. Each petal is shaped separately before assembly, which means you can vary colour and position freely. A set of two or three brooches made from the same palette looks cohesive even if the individual pieces are slightly different sizes. The pin is glued after
curing
rather than embedded in raw clay, which gives a stronger hold. These brooches are decorative pieces only.

tools.name: Pottery rolling pin or smooth glass bottle
Baking tray lined with greaseproof paper

materials.name: Polymer clay, contrasting colour for centre

content.text: Preparation

Warm the clay thoroughly in your hands until pliable. Roll a small ball of the contrasting clay for the centre bead of each flower: roughly 8-10 mm in diameter. Set these aside while you prepare the petals. Preheat the oven to 110-130 degrees C and line a small baking tray with greaseproof paper.

For each brooch, pinch off five equal portions of petal clay. Roll each into a small ball (roughly 10-12 mm), then flatten each ball between your fingers into an oval disc about 2-3 mm thick. Press one end thinner and leave the other end slightly thicker to give a petal shape with a narrow tip and a rounded base.
Press a small flat disc of clay onto your lined baking tray as a
back plate
: roll a portion of petal clay into a flat circle about 25-30 mm across and 2 mm thick. This is the foundation the petals attach to and will become the back of the brooch.

Arrange the five petals around the edge of the back plate with the narrow tips pointing outward and the rounded bases meeting in the centre. Press each petal gently but firmly against the back plate to bond them together. Overlap each petal slightly over the previous one as you work around the circle.

Press the centre bead into the middle of the flower where the petal bases meet. Flatten it slightly so it sits securely rather than perching on top. Use the needle tool to add a small indent or texture to the centre bead if you like.
Add gentle vein detail to each petal by dragging the needle tool from the base toward the tip in a single light stroke. This is optional but adds definition to the finished piece.
Bake on the lined tray at 110-130 degrees C for 20-25 minutes. Leave to cool fully inside the oven with the door ajar before removing.

Once cold, apply a small dot of strong jewellery adhesive to the brooch pin bar and press it horizontally across the upper third of the back of the brooch. Hold in place for 60 seconds then leave to cure according to the adhesive instructions, typically 2-4 hours, before wearing.
Variations
Use seven petals for a fuller, daisy-like form.
Add a second, smaller layer of five petals on top of the first, rotated so they fill the gaps between the lower petals.
Roll the centre bead in seed beads or fine glitter before pressing in place for extra texture.
Convert to hair clips or lapel pins by swapping the brooch pin finding for the appropriate hardware.

items.fix: Apply a small dot of liquid polymer clay or jewellery adhesive to the joint. Rebake if using liquid clay; leave to cure if using adhesive.
items.cause: Petals not pressed firmly enough against the base before baking
items.problem: Petals separate from the back plate after baking

items.fix: Press the centre element more firmly into the meeting point of the petals in the next piece. A slight indent made with the needle tool in the petals before adding the bead helps seat it lower.
items.cause: Bead not flattened sufficiently at the base before pressing in
items.problem: Centre bead sits too high and looks unstable

items.fix: Always position the pin in the upper third so the brooch hangs correctly and the pin bar has more clay surface behind it for the adhesive to grip.
items.cause: Pin positioned at the centre rather than the upper third of the back
items.problem: Brooch pin twists and comes away from the back

initialFindings.message: paragraph reads at grade 11.1 (qc threshold: 11) — simplify (target grade 6-8)
initialFindings.snippet: Once cold, apply a small dot of strong jewellery adhesive to the brooch pin bar and press it horizontally across the upper third of the back
